message:
feat(version-check): add background version check with 24h cache and config toggle

Introduce a new `version_check` module that provides non-blocking, cached
version comparison against GitHub releases. The check uses a simple file-
based cache (~/.config/pi/.version_check_cache) with a 24-hour TTL so
startup is never blocked by network I/O.

New module (src/version_check.rs):
- `is_newer()` compares semver strings with v-prefix and pre-release
  suffix tolerance
- `check_cached()` returns UpdateAvailable/UpToDate/Failed from cache
- `parse_github_release_version()` extracts tag_name from GH API JSON
- `read_cached_version()`/`write_cached_version()` manage the cache file
- 10 unit tests covering: basic comparison, v-prefix, pre-release,
  invalid versions, GH JSON parsing, cache round-trip, missing/empty cache

Config integration (src/config.rs):
- Add `check_for_updates: Option<bool>` field with camelCase alias
- `should_check_for_updates()` accessor defaults to true
- Merge support (other overrides base)
- 3 unit tests: default-true, explicit-false, explicit-true (snake_case)

Interactive integration (src/interactive.rs):
- On app initialization, if `should_check_for_updates()` is true,
  check the local cache (zero network) and show a status bar message
  when a newer version is available

Wiring (src/lib.rs):
- Export `pub mod version_check` from the crate root

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
